name: Ozark Folk Magic
description: Ozark Folk Magic, also known as Ozark Conjure or Hillfolk Magic, is a syncretic magical tradition rooted in the Ozark Mountain region of the United States. Its origins lie in a blend of European folk magic brought by settlers (primarily from the British Isles and Germany), Native American herbal knowledge, and African-American conjure traditions. This unique combination resulted in a practical, down-to-earth magic focused on everyday needs and problems. Ozark Folk Magic emphasizes herbalism, divination (especially through dreams and signs), and spellcrafting using readily available materials. It's characterized by its pragmatism, relying on common sense and direct action alongside spiritual interventions. The focus is often on healing, protection, love, prosperity, and settling disputes. Unlike formalized religious magic, Ozark Folk Magic is typically practiced independently or within families, passed down through oral tradition and practical demonstration. The cultural significance lies in its reflection of the Ozark people's self-reliance, resourcefulness, and deep connection to the land.
subtitle: Hillfolk Conjure and Practical Magic

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Root Medicine
keyMyths.description: A tale of how the knowledge of herbs and their healing properties was gifted to the Ozark people by benevolent spirits or through careful observation of animal behavior.

keyMyths.title: The Legend of the Water Witch
keyMyths.description: Stories of women with the ability to find water sources using divining rods or other methods, highlighting their connection to the land and their practical skills.

keyMyths.title: The Power of the Charm
keyMyths.description: Accounts of charms and amulets created to protect against harm, bring good luck, or influence events, demonstrating the belief in the power of focused intention and natural objects.

sources.author: Vance Randolph
sources.description: A classic collection of Ozark folk beliefs and practices.
